preamble: The short vowel /i/ reduces to schwa [ə] in unstressed or final syllables across multiple Arabic dialects. This is a prominent feature of Levantine and other Mesopotamian dialects where short /i/ and /u/ have merged with schwa, with very little distinction in unstressed positions.

examples.note: Complete merger of short /i/ with schwa in many Levantine dialects
Short /i/ collapses to schwa with minimal distinction in North Mesopotamian varieties

context: Unstressed or final syllables; particularly in Levantine and North Mesopotamian dialects
allophones_note: Short /i/ reduction to schwa in unstressed positions across Levantine and Mesopotamian varieties
